summary: ts-einkle-slot ships cloned big.js with covert import-time loader of unrelated `node-slot` module
details: The package advertises itself as an arbitrary-precision arithmetic library and ships the verbatim source of big.js v7.0.1 (including MikeMcl/big.js attribution), but the package name, declared dependencies, and inserted code have no relation to big.js. The main entry (big.js / big.mjs) contains an injected block that, on require/import of the package, executes `require("node-slot"); doc.from_str()` inside a try/catch that swallows all errors. `node-slot` has no relation to the advertised arithmetic purpose and is loaded covertly whenever a consumer imports the library. The package also declares an unrelated runtime dependency `ts-einkle@^1.1.0`, pulling additional code into the installer's dependency tree. The cloned-library cover, the covertly invoked unrelated module on import, and the silenced error handling together form a payload-staging trojan: any consumer that installs and requires ts-einkle-slot executes whatever `node-slot` / `ts-einkle` contain on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
